entries.definition:
r–t–q space between any two fingers; a closed up mass,
darkness; to mend, to join together, to repair, to patch up; sticking
together. Of this root, رَتْق ratq occurs once in the Qur’an.

رَتْق ratq [n.; v. n.] layers pressed together into one mass,
closed up mass (21:30) أَوَلَمْ يَرَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا أَنَّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ كَانَتَا رَتْقًا

347
